parameters.content:
### Step 3: Save to Google Sheet
⚙️ Add **Google Sheets OAuth2** credential and select your spreadsheet.
⚙️ Add Sheet ID and Sheet name (Transactions)

### Step 2: AI Categorization
⚙️ Add **Google Gemini API** credential.

### Step 1: Receive & Parse
No configuration needed.

## 📱 Auto Expense Tracker with FlowTrigger

Automatically tracks expenses by capturing banking push notifications from your phone using the [FlowTrigger](https://flowtrigger.app) app, categorizing them with AI (Google Gemini), and logging everything to a Google Sheet.

---

### Setup


**1. Add credentials to `Google Gemini Chat Model:**
 Add your Google Gemini API key ([Get one here](https://aistudio.google.com/apikey))


**2. Connect your spreadsheet**
- Copy the Google Sheets Template -> [Copy](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1SINQnhu4slpNsVZ-CF8LqhTDKpqPqS1gukQcFlRWJXs/copy)
- Open `Google Sheets` node :
   - Connect Google Sheets OAuth2 account
   - Select the copied spreadsheet → select the **Transactions** sheet


**3. Get the Webhook URL**
Activate this workflow → open the `Webhook` node → copy the **Production URL**


**4. Set up FlowTrigger**
- Install [FlowTrigger](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=app.flowtrigger) on your Android phone
- Create a **Notification Trigger**
- Paste the Webhook URL
- Select your banking app (e.g. Google Wallet, Revolut, your bank) as the notification source
- Done! Expenses are now tracked automatically 🎉

---

### How it works
1. Your banking app sends a payment notification → FlowTrigger captures it and sends the data to this workflow
2. The workflow extracts the amount and date, then AI categorizes the merchant and expense type
3. The categorized expense is saved to your Google Sheet

For more details visit [flowtrigger.app](https://flowtrigger.app)"

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ status: "error", source: "google_sheets", message: ($json.error ? ($json.error.message || JSON.stringify($json.error)) : "Failed to write transaction to Google Sheets.") }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ status: "success", data: { merchant: $('Categorize Expense').item.json.merchant_cleaned, category: $('Categorize Expense').item.json.category, amount: $('Extract Amount').item.json.extractedAmount, currency: $('Categorize Expense').item.json.currency, date: $('Extract Amount').item.json.formattedDate } }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ status: "error", source: "categorization", message: ($json.error ? ($json.error.message || JSON.stringify($json.error)) : "AI categorization failed. The LLM did not return a valid response.") }) }}

parameters.text:
=You are a precise financial data extraction bot.

Merchant Text: {{ $json.body.notificationTitle }}
Context Text: {{ $json.body.notificationBody }}

STEP 1 - ANALYZE PAYLOAD:
- Read full notification body and title content. You should only look for expense notifications. Sometimes banking apps send informational notifications to users. Sometimes notification is about money return or other income - look for clues in the content. If there is any sign that the notification might not be an expense ignore it. You should only analyze payment related notifications, ignore the rest. Expense notifications often have just merchant in title and price + card details in body.

STEP 2 — IDENTIFY THE MERCHANT:
- Strip store numbers, location suffixes, and branch codes from the Merchant Text to extract the clean brand or business name.
- Determine what kind of business this merchant is. If it is a well-known retail chain, supermarket, restaurant, pharmacy, gas station, etc., use your knowledge of that business to choose the category.

STEP 3 — DETERMINE REGION:
- Infer the country from the language and currency in Context Text.

STEP 4 — CATEGORIZE:
- Map to EXACTLY ONE category:🛒 Groceries,🏥 Health,🚗 Transport,🍽️ Dining,⚡ Utilities,🛍️ Shopping,👠 Entertainment,📦 Other.
- Supermarkets and discount grocery stores are "Groceries", not "Dining" — even if they also sell prepared food.
- If you do not recognize the merchant, reason carefully from the name and regional context. Do NOT guess based on partial word translations.

STEP 5 — CURRENCY:
- Output the 3-letter ISO currency code.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ status: "skipped", message: "No price detected. Non-expense notification ignored." }) }}
